sections.paragraph:
ὑποδέχομαι mid. dep.; fut. 3 pl. ὑποδέξονται 4 Macc 13:17; 1 aor. ὑπεδεξάμην; pf. ὑποδέδεγμαι (Hom. et al.; ins [e.g. IG X/2, 255, 5; 17], pap, LXX; TestSol 16:2; TestAbr; Philo) to receive hospitably, receive, welcome, entertain as a guest τινά someone (POslo 55, 8 [c. 200 a.d.]; TestAbr 2 p. 78, 20 [Stone p. 4]; Jos., Ant. 1, 180 al.) Lk 10:38; 19:6; Ac 17:7; Js 2:25; ISm 10:1; AcPl Ant 13, 10 (=Aa I 237, 1). τινὰ εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν (Chion, Ep. 2 εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν ὑποδέχεσθαι αὐτόν) Lk 10:38 v.l. (s. οἰκία; RBorger TRu 52, ’87, 32f); cp. Hs 8, 10, 3; 9, 27, 2 (ὑπ. τινα εἰς as 1 Macc 16:15).—M-M.

dep. dep. = deponent

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pl. = plural

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

POslo POslo = Papyri Osloenses—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

AcPl Ant AcPl Ant = Acts of Paul, PAntinoopolis 13—Lists 1, 4

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

Chion Chion , IV b.c.; for letters attributed to him, I a.d., s. Ep.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

TRu TRu = Theologische Rundschau—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1037
